{"id":"ox-eoc","title":"Fresh install UX: init then doctor should not show warnings","description":"Epic: Users doing ox init on an existing repo immediately see ox doctor issues. Build E2E test framework, capture what breaks, research daemon-in-init, fix issues.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:04:47Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:04:47Z"}

{"id":"ox-6fk","title":"Build E2E test framework for fresh install flow","description":"Create doctor_e2e_test.go and doctor_e2e_helpers_test.go with integration build tag. Two variants: mock server (no creds) and real infra (steveyegge/beads). Build ox binary, run ox init + ox doctor as subprocesses, parse JSONDoctorOutput, produce fresh install report.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:04:55Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:04:55Z"}

{"id":"ox-lav","title":"Separate team discovery from credential token refresh in daemon","description":"Daemon conflates token validity with team membership discovery. New teams invisible until token nears expiry. Fix: add discoverTeams() that re-fetches repo list on separate 5-min cadence. GH issue #39.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:07:02Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:07:02Z"}

{"id":"ox-oer","title":"Run tests and capture fresh install doctor report","description":"Execute the E2E tests (mock server variant). Capture and analyze the full FreshInstallReport showing all warnings, failures, and skipped checks that appear after ox init without ox sync. This report drives the fix decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:04Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:04Z"}

new_value: Implemented multi-phrasing (5 variants per query), replaced arch-decisions with project-guidance, added attribution-guidance and subagent-discovery queries, tightened validators, bumped iterations to 5. All tests pass.

Report captured. Findings: 1 failure (git repo paths - team context dir not found), 2 unexpected warnings (.sageox/ unstaged, 17 uncommitted changes). Root cause: init creates files but daemon not running to clone team contexts, and user hasn't committed yet.

old_value: {"id":"ox-0qf","title":"Research: should daemon start as part of ox init?","description":"After seeing test results, investigate: Could ox init call autoStartDaemon() + RequestSync()? Should doctor detect just-initialized state and suppress warnings? Review IPC architecture constraints. Compare tradeoffs (latency vs UX cleanliness).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:05Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:05Z"}

new_value: Research complete. IPC architecture doc (line 29) already specifies init should start daemon. Current code only requests sync if daemon already running. Three issues found: (1) git repo paths fails because team context dir not cloned, (2) .sageox/ changes warning because files uncommitted, (3) uncommitted changes warning. Fix: add autoStartDaemon() + RequestSync() to init, OR make doctor's git repo paths check tolerate not-yet-synced state.

old_value: {"id":"ox-awe","title":"Fix identified fresh install issues","description":"Based on findings from E2E tests and daemon research, implement fixes. Options include: starting daemon in init, making doctor bootstrapping-aware based on init timestamp, or combination. Ensure ox init + ox doctor flow produces zero unexpected warnings.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:05Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:05:05Z"}

new_value: Separated team discovery from credential token refresh. New discoverTeams() method re-fetches repo list every 5 min independent of token expiry.

Both fixes implemented: (A) init now auto-starts daemon and requests sync, (B) doctor git repo paths check tolerates recently-initialized state. E2E test passes with 0 failures, 0 unexpected warnings.
Epic complete. E2E test framework built, issues identified and fixed: init starts daemon, doctor graceful about bootstrapping.

Fix all four issues: wrong check file, incomplete hook detection, ClaudeAgent.List() wrong file, and add doctor --fix for stale hooks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:35:42Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:35:42Z"}

old_value: {"id":"ox-e7m","title":"Fix team-discussions: agent never runs ox agent team-ctx (0/5 benchmark)","description":"Benchmark shows 0/5 success for team-discussions across all 5 prompt variants. The agent reads AGENTS.md (which lists the command) but never actually runs 'ox agent team-ctx'. Instead it tries git log, ls, Glob, ox status — everything except the right command. Root cause: prime output lists team-ctx as an 'available command' but doesn't instruct the agent to USE it when asked about team discussions. Fix options: (1) Add a stronger hint in prime output like 'To access team discussions, run: ox agent team-ctx' (2) Add a suggested_commands field to prime output (3) Make the AGENTS.md fixture more directive. This is the biggest benchmark gap — team discussion retrieval is a core ox value prop.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:28Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:28Z"}

{"id":"ox-q8o","title":"Reduce sageox-issues tool calls: agent takes 3-5 calls to find ox doctor","description":"Benchmark shows 5/5 success but median 5 tool calls before finding ox doctor. The agent explores (ls, Read, Glob) before reaching for the right command. Prime output should make 'ox doctor' more prominent as THE health check command. Consider: (1) Adding 'For health checks, run: ox doctor' to prime output (2) Making ox doctor the first suggested command when issues are suspected. Currently the agent wastes 2-4 calls exploring before finding the answer.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:28Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:28Z"}

{"id":"ox-2cm","title":"Fix sageox-sync: vague phrasings fail (2/5 benchmark failures)","description":"Benchmark shows 3/5 success for sageox-sync. Failing variants: 'Is the team context up to date?' (agent checks ~/.claude/teams/ instead of ox status) and 'Are we seeing the latest team discussions or is the sync stale?' (agent runs bd sync/bd list — confusing beads sync with ox sync). Root cause: (1) Agent doesn't associate 'up to date' or 'stale' with ox sync commands (2) The word 'sync' in the prompt triggers beads sync instead of ox sync. Fix: prime output should explicitly map sync-related concepts to 'ox status' or 'ox sync' commands.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:29Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:29Z"}

{"id":"ox-av8","title":"Fix session-history v0: direct phrasing fails","description":"Benchmark shows 4/5 success for session-history but v0 'Show me the session history for this project' failed — agent only did 'ls -la' (1 tool call) and stopped. The other 4 indirect phrasings all succeeded (the agent tried git log or ox session). The most direct, obvious phrasing is the one that fails. Investigate whether prime output should include a stronger hint for 'ox session list' or whether the validator needs to accept 'ls' as a first exploratory step.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:29Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:32:29Z"}

old_value: {"id":"ox-c4j","title":"Reorder init next-steps and fix uncertain wording","description":"Gap 2: After ox init, the final step says 'Claude Code should auto-run: ox agent prime' — 'should' signals uncertainty. Also ox doctor is step 4 (after inviting teammates) but verification should come before social actions. Fix: (1) Change 'should auto-run' to 'will auto-run'. (2) Move ox doctor to step 2 (right after commit/push). (3) Combine 'Start Claude Code' and 'auto-run prime' into one step: 'Start Claude Code — ox will auto-prime and surface your team context'. File: cmd/ox/init.go:760-770","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:15Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:15Z"}

{"id":"ox-8bb","title":"Show team context location in init next-steps","description":"Gap 3: After ox init, user is never told WHERE team context will live or that it's syncing in background. If daemon sync hasn't completed (2s timeout), user has no visibility. Fix: After the next-steps list, add a brief 'What happens next' section showing team context sync path and suggesting ox status to check progress. File: cmd/ox/init.go:718-777","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:19Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:19Z"}

{"id":"ox-i7c","title":"Surface daemon sync timeout during init","description":"Gap 4: If daemon doesn't become healthy within 2 seconds during init, it logs to slog.Debug and moves on silently. User gets no indication sync is pending/delayed. The conditional only shows 'Run ox sync' if sync completely failed, but the 2s timeout case is silently ignored. Fix: When daemon sync times out, show a brief note: 'Background sync starting... run ox status to check progress.' File: cmd/ox/init.go:672-707","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:23Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:23Z"}

{"id":"ox-e7q","title":"Add team context readiness state to prime output","description":"Gap 5: When team context directory doesn't exist yet (daemon still syncing), prime returns team_context: null with no hint it will appear soon. Agent has no way to tell user 'check back in a moment.' Fix: When team context path is computed but directory is empty/missing, include a team_context_status: 'syncing' field so agents can say 'Team context is syncing, will be available shortly.' File: cmd/ox/agent_prime.go (team context discovery section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:27Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:27Z"}

Hooks: active | Daemon: running | Team context: synced'. Files: cmd/ox/doctor.go or cmd/ox/doctor_hooks.go","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:30Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:23:30Z"}

new_value: Added checkUploadAccess() call in runAgentSessionStart() before recording begins. Returns clear error for read-only viewers.

old_value: {"id":"ox-vnt","title":"Auto-recover ghost sessions on agent prime","description":"Bug: When user exits Claude Code without running /ox-session-stop (e.g., after being told to restart for hooks), the .recording.json persists. On next launch with a new agent ID, ox session list shows nothing (queries ledger) but /ox-session-start fails with 'already recording' (finds orphaned local state). Fix: During ox agent prime (or at session start), check if existing .recording.json belongs to the current Claude session. If not (different agent ID or session marker), auto-recover or clear it. Key files: internal/session/recording.go (LoadRecordingState returns first .recording.json without agent ID filtering), cmd/ox/agent_session.go:90 (only checks if ANY session exists), cmd/ox/prime_session_marker.go (doesn't clean old sessions). The 24-hour stale threshold in doctor is too long for this scenario — ghost sessions from a hooks restart happen within minutes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:32:31Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:32:31Z"}

new_value: Ghost sessions from different agent IDs are now auto-cleared on session start

{"id":"ox-cu7","title":"Add LastSyncForPath helper to StatusData","description":"Create a shared helper method on daemon.StatusData that looks up a workspace's last sync time by path. This eliminates the repeated nested-loop pattern (iterating daemonStatus.Workspaces to find a match by path) used inline in status.go. Signature: func (s *StatusData) LastSyncForPath(path string) (time.Time, bool). {"id":"ox-a9y","title":"Session recording captures pre-start content and truncates at plan exit","description":"## Bug Summary

Session recording for 2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO has three issues:

1. **Pre-session content included**: raw.jsonl starts at seq=0 with "switch to a new branch" — 3 entries BEFORE /ox-session-start was called (seq=3). The recording should only include content from AFTER ox agent session start completes.

2. **Session truncated at plan mode exit**: Recording ends at seq=32 "[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" which corresponds to ExitPlanMode. The entire implementation phase is missing: parallel subagent execution (3 agents), code changes to 4 files, build/lint/test verification, beads issue management, git commit, git push, and PR creation. This is ~80% of the session value.

3. **Plan content not captured**: The plan was written to a file via the Write tool but the plan content itself doesn't appear in raw.jsonl (tool entries show [0 chars]). Similarly, all subagent results are absent.

## Expected Behavior

- raw.jsonl should NOT include any entries from before ox agent session start completes
- raw.jsonl should include ALL entries from session start up to (but not including) ox agent session stop  
- Tool results (especially Write, Task subagent results) should have their content captured, not [0 chars]

## Observed Behavior

- 33 entries in raw.jsonl, 3 of which predate session start
- Recording stops at ExitPlanMode (seq=32), missing the entire implementation
- Most tool results show [0 chars] content

## Reproduction

Session: 2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO
Ledger path: ledger/sessions/2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO/
Local cache: Library/Caches/sageox/sessions/repo_019c5812.../sessions/2026-02-19T15-48-ryan-Ox91IO/

## Investigation Starting Points

- How does the session recorder hook into Claude Code's message stream?
- Is there a message buffer that gets flushed at session start (explaining pre-start content)?
- What causes the recording to stop mid-session? Is there a max entry count, a context compaction event, or does ExitPlanMode somehow signal an end?
- Why are tool results empty ([0 chars])? Is this intentional filtering or a bug in content extraction?","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:25:55Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:25:55Z"}

Should support --force flag for non-interactive use (registered on agentCmd, same pattern as abort). Needs confirmation prompt for interactive terminals.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T23:44:22Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T23:44:22Z"}

Config location: .sageox/config.json or user config. Relevant files: cmd/ox/agent_session_recover.go, internal/doctor/session.go.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-18T13:03:17Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-18T13:03:17Z"}

{"id":"ox-ucv","title":"Add 'ox session regenerate' command for HTML re-generation","description":"Add a CLI command to regenerate session HTML from existing JSONL and re-upload to LFS, overwriting the previous HTML.

**Context**: During development of the session HTML viewer, we need to periodically regenerate HTML for existing sessions to test template changes against real data. Currently this requires 3 manual steps: (1) delete cached HTML, (2) ox session view --html to regenerate, (3) ox session upload to push new HTML to LFS.

**Desired UX**:
```
ox session regenerate <name>           # regenerate one session
ox session regenerate --all            # regenerate all sessions
```

**What it should do**:
1. Find the session's JSONL (from ledger or cache)
2. Delete any existing cached session.html
3. Call generateHTML() to produce new HTML from JSONL + summary.json
4. Upload the new HTML to LFS via uploadSessionLFS(), overwriting the old blob
5. Update meta.json with new file refs
6. Commit and push to ledger

**Key files**:
- cmd/ox/session_upload_cmd.go — existing upload logic to reuse
- cmd/ox/session_html.go — generateHTML() for HTML generation
- cmd/ox/session_view_html.go — viewHTMLGenerate() shows how view delegates to generateHTML

**Notes**:
- This is a development convenience command, may be removed once we have a web-based viewer
- Should work with --all flag to batch regenerate (useful after template changes)
- summary.json should also be re-enriched with chapter data during regeneration
- Consider a --force flag to skip 'are you sure' confirmation on --all","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-26T23:50:37Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-26T23:50:37Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Add a CLI command that validates session JSONL files and reports issues.

**Context**: Session recordings come from different ox versions, agent adapters, and external imports. Older sessions may have schema inconsistencies, missing fields, or content patterns that degrade HTML rendering quality. Currently there's no way to check a JSONL file for conformance issues.

**Critical use case**: This linter MUST be used as a validation gate in `ox session import` before accepting external JSONL into the system. Import should reject JSONL that fails lint with errors (warnings OK).

**Desired UX**:
```
ox session lint <name>         # lint one session by name
ox session lint --file foo.jsonl  # lint a JSONL file (for pre-import validation)
ox session lint --all          # lint all sessions in ledger
```

**What it should check**:
- Header entry present with required metadata fields (version, agent_id, agent_type)
- Sequential seq numbers without gaps
- Valid timestamp format on all entries
- Known type values (user, assistant, tool, system, tool_result, info, header)
- Tool entries have tool_name field
- Tool entries with tool_input have parseable JSON
- Content not empty for user/assistant entries (warn on empty)
- Preamble noise patterns that stripPreamble would filter (/ox-session-start, system-reminder, ox-hash markers)
- Duplicate seq numbers
- Entries after session footer
- Excessively large entries (>1MB content)
- Valid JSON on every line (critical for import)

**Integration with ox session import**:
- Extract validation logic into a reusable `internal/session/lint.go` package
- `ox session import` calls the linter before writing to ledger
- Import rejects on errors, warns on warnings
- Lint results returned as structured data for programmatic use

**Output**: Summary report with counts (errors, warnings, info) and per-entry details for issues found. JSON output mode for programmatic use.

**Key files**:
- internal/session/entry.go — existing entry type mapping
- internal/session/html/grouping.go — preamble/noise detection patterns to reuse
- cmd/ox/session_html.go — buildMessageView shows what fields the HTML generator expects"}

- Content not empty for user/assistant entries (warn on empty)
- Preamble noise patterns that stripPreamble would filter (/ox-session-start, system-reminder, ox-hash markers)

- Entries after session footer
- Excessively large entries (>1MB content)

- internal/session/entry.go — existing entry type mapping

{"id":"ox-9lc","title":"Implement LLM redaction pass for observations","description":"Before any observation is written to disk, run LLM redaction. {"id":"ox-jip","title":"Add per-step timing instrumentation to ox agent prime","description":"ox agent prime has no latency metrics. When it runs slow (e.g., 25s observed), there's no way to diagnose which step was slow. Add slog.Debug timing for each phase of runAgentPrime (daemon health check, session marker reads, auth check, instance store ops, session recording, team context discovery, guidance load, daemon attachment). Include total elapsed time in JSON output. Friction telemetry captures errors but not latency - this fills that gap.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T22:43:42Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-16T22:43:42Z"}

new_value: All 4 sub-issues closed: ox-2cm (sageox-sync), ox-av8 (session-history), ox-e7m (team-discussions), ox-q8o (reduce tool calls)

Implemented two-phase materialization in Checkout(). Phase 1 clones with --filter=blob:none --depth=1 --sparse --no-checkout to fetch only tree objects, then materializes .sageox/ to read the manifest. Phase 2 computes sparse paths from the manifest and materializes only declared includes. Uses --no-cone mode to support both file and directory patterns. Includes self-healing for incomplete clones, validation of materialized content, and unshallow for subsequent pulls. 7 new tests all passing.
Implemented sync state tracking, staleness detection in ox status and ox agent prime

{"description":"Periodic GC via fresh reclone on gc_interval_days cadence (default 7, min 1, max 90). Also triggers immediate reclone when a team context is detected as a full clone (missing extensions.partialClone in git config) — this ensures existing users upgrade to partial clones without waiting for GC interval.

Reclone triggers:
1. gc_interval_days exceeded (normal GC)
2. Full clone detected (upgrade path for pre-v4 installs)

Procedure: 1) verify clean working state (skip if uncommitted changes), 2) full two-phase materialization into TEAM_DIR.new, 3) validate new clone (manifest present, key files materialized, sparse set matches), 4) atomic swap (mv old, mv new, update symlinks), 5) rm -rf old. Error handling: clone fails = keep old + retry next cycle, validation fails = keep old + remove new + log, swap fails = keep old + log. Affects: internal/daemon/. Spec section 4.

Detection: use 'git -C <path> config --get extensions.partialClone' — empty/error means full clone. This is the same check used by 'ox doctor' (checkTeamContextCloneStrategy)."}

{"id":"ox-aa2","title":"Make doctor warnings actionable for non-technical users","description":"User feedback: 'I am not sure how to fix the warning about cloud doctor, or adding AGENTS.md at the team level.' Doctor output shows warnings like 'Team twnstr-team: AGENTS.md (missing)' with a raw file path but no clear next step for a user who doesn't know what AGENTS.md is or why it matters. Similarly, 'Cloud doctor (unavailable)' gives no guidance. Fix: Add brief remediation hints to doctor warnings. For AGENTS.md missing: explain what it does and offer to create a starter template (ox doctor --fix). For cloud doctor unavailable: explain this is normal if the cloud service is temporarily unreachable and is non-critical. Key files: cmd/ox/doctor_hooks.go, doctor check registration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-02-23T21:32:34Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:32:34Z"}

new_value: Improved AGENTS.md and Cloud doctor warnings with user-friendly explanations, consequences, and actionable next steps
Implemented staleness detection in agent_prime.go and status.go. Daemon IPC with local config fallback, 24h threshold, warnings in prime text output and status JSON.
Implemented ox memory distill command with observation scanning, API client, state tracking, --dry-run, --since, --manifest flags. Tests pass.
Added per-step timing to runAgentPrime: daemon_health, session_start, team_context, guidance_build phases. ElapsedMs and Timing map in JSON output.
All 4 child issues already closed (ox-2cm, ox-av8, ox-e7m, ox-q8o). Epic complete.

Read pipeline epic complete. Prime v4 loading (ox-221), staleness detection (ox-dnv), memory distill command (ox-pp8), per-step timing (ox-jip), and benchmark improvements (ox-bks) all implemented and closed.

Implemented blue-green reclone GC. checkAndRunGC() runs on hourly ticker, checks each team context workspace against gc_interval_days from manifest. runBlueGreenGC() performs 5-step procedure: verify clean state, two-phase clone into .new, validate, atomic swap (rename old→.old, new→current), cleanup. validateGCClone() enforces .git, .sageox, and core files. WorkspaceState tracks GCIntervalDays and LastGCTime. 8 new tests all passing.
